item.title: cmd_run treats unknown flags as item IDs instead of erroring
item.intent: cmd_run must reject unrecognized flags — silently accepting unknown options as item IDs causes confusing behavior where typos like --dry_run are interpreted as sprint IDs and silently do nothing
item.description: cmd_run() has a catch-all in its option parsing loop (around line 1905) that accepts any non-flag positional argument as either a count or a specific item ID. This means unknown flags like '--dry_run' (intended as --dry-run) or '--retries' (missing value) are silently interpreted as item IDs, producing a 'no item found' error rather than 'unknown option'. The fix is to detect that an argument starts with '--' in the catch-all and reject it as an unknown option before treating it as a positional.
steps: In .aishore/aishore, find cmd_run() and locate the catch-all in the option parsing loop (around line 1905-1912)
In the catch-all branch, before the existing logic, add: if [[ "$1" == --* ]]; then log_error "Unknown option: $1"; return 1; fi
Verify that 'run 3', 'run FEAT-001', and 'run' without arguments still work correctly
Verify that 'run --typo' now prints an error and exits non-zero
acceptanceCriteria: aishore run --unknown-flag exits with a non-zero status and prints an error message
aishore run N (numeric count), aishore run <ID>, and aishore run still work correctly
Error message matches the format used by other commands: 'Unknown option: <flag>'
